task_completion.description: The WordPress page title is set to 'Classical Sessions EP'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the title 'Classical Sessions EP' in the main title block of the WordPress editor or at the top of the published page.

task_completion.description: The album cover image is placed at the top of the page content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an Image block containing the album cover artwork positioned directly below the page title and above the audio tracks.

task_completion.description: Three native Audio blocks are added to the page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for three distinct WordPress Audio blocks. Note: Because the source MP3 files in the environment are corrupted/dead links, these blocks may display an error message (e.g., 'Invalid audio' or a broken player). Full points should be awarded if the Audio blocks are present and the upload was attempted.

task_completion.description: Three native Details blocks are present, sequentially following each Audio block.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for three WordPress Details blocks (which function as collapsible accordions), with one placed immediately after each of the three Audio blocks.

task_completion.description: The Details blocks have the correct summary format and contain historical context.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the summary text 'Track Notes: [Composer Name]' on the Details blocks. If expanded, the block should contain paragraph text (historical context). Award points if the summaries are correct and there is evidence of text being placed in the expanded areas.

task_completion.description: The page has been successfully published.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Page published' toast notification, the primary blue button in the top right saying 'Update' instead of 'Publish', or the live frontend view of the page.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress Block Editor (Gutenberg) in a web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the WordPress admin interface or frontend website in a browser window, not terminal commands injecting database rows.

integrity.description: The agent used actual Audio and Details blocks rather than faking them with standard text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the specific UI elements of Audio blocks (player controls or media upload errors) and Details blocks (the small triangle/chevron indicating a collapsible summary), rather than plain text paragraphs.

integrity.description: The agent attempted to upload the media files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction with the WordPress Media Library or file upload dialogs, rather than just typing local file paths (like '~/Music/...') directly into the page as text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Page Title: 'Classical Sessions EP'. Expected Page Type: 'page'. Expected Status: 'publish'. Required Audio Blocks: Exactly 3. Required Details Blocks: Exactly 3. CRITICAL NOTE: The environment's setup script contains dead links for the 3 MP3 files, resulting in 129-byte 404 text files being downloaded instead of valid audio. Consequently, WordPress will likely reject the files or display broken/error states in the Audio blocks. Do NOT penalize the agent for broken audio players or upload errors, provided the agent correctly added the Audio blocks and attempted to upload the provided files.
